data.notes: The worker is healthy and nearly done — the transcript shows the correct one-line edit to consult.ts (`cfg.consult.stallMinutes * 60_000`) already applied and the stub-driver test actively being written when the 15m stall fired; this is slowness, not a hang or the earlier --trust defect (now fixed). Retry on the same assignment: the worktree likely still contains the prior edit, so first check git status/log there and keep any existing work rather than redoing it, then finish tests/run/consult.test.ts, run the suite, and commit atomically. Critical: end with the TICKMARKR_RESULT trailer as the final line with a very short summary (<10 words) — cursor's renderer hard-wraps long lines and a wrapped trailer parses as garbage (that is exactly what killed the 06:39 attempt), so brevity is what gets this task through the gate.

data.notes: This is cursor-agent-specific failure, not a task problem: two consecutive attempts on composer-2.5 died — one to the renderer's hard-wrapped trailer parsing as garbage, this one to sheer slowness (15m spent on a one-line edit plus a partial test, still mid-edit when the stall fired). The task is small and crisply specified, so route it to a different CLI (claude-code, frontier or mid tier) whose renderer doesn't wrap the trailer. Note the worktree is recreated fresh on each dispatch, wiping uncommitted progress — instruct the new worker to commit the consult.ts one-liner immediately as its first atomic commit, then write the stub-driver test, run the suite, commit, and end with a TICKMARKR_RESULT trailer whose summary is under 10 words.

data.notes: Harness defect, not a worker problem: all three worker-results (06:39:03, 06:40:30, 06:41:44) landed 30–45s after dispatch with 'unparseable TICKMARKR_RESULT trailer', finished:false, exitCode:null — physically impossible completions, on two different vendors. Root cause: the retry/reroute consult notes embedded in the worker prompt contain the literal string 'TICKMARKR_RESULT', so the pane echo of the prompt matches the trailer regex, parses as garbage, and fails closed seconds after launch — exactly the 'first regex match of a trailer' anti-pattern. The current 15m stall shows a healthy worker with the correct consult.ts edit already applied, but each re-dispatch recreates the worktree and wipes it. Do not retry/reroute — every path re-enters the same broken parse. Operator must (1) sanitize consult-feedback text before embedding it in prompts (or make the trailer parser reject matches that appear before worker output / whose JSON doesn't parse, continuing to wait instead of failing), and (2) optionally just land P01-03 by hand — it is one line in consult.ts (cfg.consult.stallMinutes * 60_000) plus a stub-driver test, minutes of work.

data.summary: Added TickmarkrConfigSchema + ConfigError with             post-merge validation in loadConfig, inferred TickmarkrConfig type, and tests provingloud enum/prefix failures plus tombstone round-trip.
All 7 git/merge operand sites now use shq(), thereplaceAll hack is gone, and real-git tests prove injection is closed and apostrophesround-trip.

data.details:
✓ loadConfig validates the merged config against TickmarkrConfigSchema and throws ConfigError(z.prettifyError(...)) on failure; TickmarkrConfig is inferred; the build is clean.: loadConfig safeParses the merged config and throws new ConfigError(z.prettifyError(r.error)); TickmarkrConfig = z.infer<typeof TickmarkrConfigSchema> replaces the hand-written interface; tsc build completed with no errors.
✓ The suite proves loud, key-named failure for every silently-corrupting value, HARD-03 charset enforcement (trailing slash allowed), and that tombstones + defaults still round-trip — HARD-02 + HARD-03 met.: New describe block covers bad tier, driver, channel, visibility.llm (each asserting the key name appears in the message), four rejected prefix variants plus two accepted trailing-slash prefixes, and a defaults round-trip; the pre-existing v1.1 tombstone tests remain and all 18 config tests pass.
✓ loadConfig on a config whose tier value is `frontierr` throws ConfigError, and the message names the offending key path (e.g. contains `frontierr` and the tiers path): TierEnum carries a custom error embedding JSON.stringify(iss.input) so the message contains "frontierr", and prettifyError includes the path; the test asserts toContain("frontierr") and toMatch(/tiers\.codex\.models/) and passes.
✓ loadConfig on a config whose driver / channel / visibility value is invalid throws ConfigError with a z.prettifyError message naming the key: Tests for driver: podman, tiers.codex.channel: grpc, and visibility.llm: verbose each assert ConfigError is thrown and the message contains the key name (driver/channel/llm) via the prettified path; all pass.
✓ loadConfig on a config whose integrationBranchPrefix contains a space, single quote, `..`, or leading `/` throws ConfigError; `tickmarkr/` and `feat/x/` (trailing slash) are accepted: Schema regex /^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9._/-]*$/ rejects spaces, quotes, and leading slash; a .refine rejects embedded '..'; test.each covers all four rejects and both trailing-slash accepts, and all pass.
✓ loadConfig on DEFAULT_CONFIG returns a value deep-equal to DEFAULT_CONFIG; the v1.1 null-tombstone overlay still deletes gpt-5.2-codex and keeps siblings: New test asserts TickmarkrConfigSchema.parse(DEFAULT_CONFIG) and loadConfig on empty dirs both deep-equal DEFAULT_CONFIG; the pre-existing v1.1 tombstone tests are untouched and green in the same run (18/18).

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- Verified independently: npm run build clean, full suite 166/167 passed (1 skipped, pre-existing), config suite 18/18 — all acceptance criteria are exercised by real tests (frontierr names tiers.codex.models path, driver/channel/llm named, prefix charset incl. trailing-slash accepts, DEFAULT_CONFIG + null-tombstone round-trip).
- Non-blocking: schema is in zod strip mode, so an unknown/typo'd key (e.g. 'concurency: 12') is silently dropped and the default silently wins — a .strict() or .loose-with-warning pass would make top-level typos loud too.
- Non-blocking: routing.map/routing.floors keys were loosened from Shape to z.string(), and pricing keys from Record<Tier,...> to z.string() — a typo'd shape or pricing-tier key validates fine and is silently ignored (z.record(TierEnum, z.number()) would enforce exhaustive, valid pricing keys).
- Non-blocking: integrationBranchPrefix regex permits some git-invalid names ('a//b', 'a.lock', trailing '.') — charset criterion (HARD-03) is met; git itself will reject the rest, so shell-safety is not affected.

2 commit(s):
src/run/git.ts          |  7 ++++---
 src/run/merge.ts        |  9 +++++----
 tests/run/git.test.ts   | 12 ++++++++++++
 tests/run/merge.test.ts | 24 +++++++++++++++++++++++-
 4 files changed, 44 insertions(+), 8 deletions(-)

✓ All 7 operand sites use bare ${shq(...)}, merge.ts has no .replaceAll, npm run build is clean, and pre-existing git/merge tests remain green.: Diff shows exactly 7 operand sites converted to bare ${shq(...)}: git.ts createWorktree (branch, dir, baseRef), removeWorktree (dir in both worktree-remove and rm -rf); merge.ts ensureIntegration (rev-parse refs/heads/${shq(branch)}, worktree add dir/branch, worktree add -b branch/dir/baseRef), mergeTask (taskBranch, message). shq is exported from src/adapters/types.ts:34 and imported in both files. grep confirms zero .replaceAll in merge.ts. Verified: `npm run build` (tsc) exits clean; vitest run of tests/run/git.test.ts + tests/run/merge.test.ts passes 11/11 including all pre-existing tests.
✓ The suite proves (a) a shell-metacharacter operand never creates the PWNED sentinel and (b) an apostrophe title round-trips into the commit subject — HARD-01 closed, independent of the HARD-03 charset gate.: Three new real-git fixture tests exist and pass: git.test.ts injects branch "tickmarkr/run'; touch PWNED #" through createWorktree and asserts no PWNED file; merge.test.ts injects taskBranch "x'; touch PWNED #" through mergeTask asserting r.ok===false and no PWNED in intWt or repo; merge.test.ts commits a merge with message "tickmarkr: merge T1 don't panic" and asserts `git log -1 --format=%s` output contains don't. These exercise the runtime functions directly with no charset pre-filtering, so they are independent of any HARD-03 gate.
✓ A shell-metacharacter payload ('; touch PWNED #) passed to createWorktree or mergeTask never creates a file named PWNED in the repo: Both injection tests use that exact payload against createWorktree and mergeTask respectively and assert existsSync(...PWNED)===false; both pass against real git repos (makeRepo fixture).
✓ A merge whose commit title contains an apostrophe succeeds and preserves it: git log -1 --format=%s on the integration worktree contains the substring don't: New test creates a real task branch, merges with message containing don't, asserts r.ok===true and shOk("git log -1 --format=%s", intWt) output contains "don't" — passes; shOk returns stdout so toContain applies to the commit subject.
✓ merge.ts no longer strips apostrophes — the lossy .replaceAll hack is deleted: Diff deletes message.replaceAll("'", "") replacing it with ${shq(message)}; grep of the current merge.ts confirms no .replaceAll remains anywhere in the file.

✓ consult's waitOutput deadline is `cfg.consult.stallMinutes * 60_000`, no `10 * 60_000` literal remains, and a timing-independent stub-driver test proves the deadline tracks the configured value — HARD-04 met.: Diff replaces the literal with cfg.consult.stallMinutes * 60_000 at the sole waitOutput call; grep of src/run/consult.ts shows the only 60_000 occurrence is the config-driven one. The new test uses a stub ExecutorDriver that captures the ms argument instead of waiting, so it is timing-independent, and the full test file passes (4/4).
✓ The consult wait deadline equals cfg.consult.stallMinutes * 60_000 — setting stallMinutes to 3 makes waitOutput receive 180000, not the hardcoded 600000: The test sets cfg.consult.stallMinutes = 3 and asserts capturedMs === 180_000 and !== 600_000, then re-checks with stallMinutes = 1 → 60_000; verified passing via vitest run.
✓ consult.ts contains no `10 * 60_000` literal: grep -n '60_000' src/run/consult.ts returns only line 72 with cfg.consult.stallMinutes * 60_000; no 10 * 60_000 literal remains.
